title: agent-ready: jjf ready returns the next unblocked issue
body:
# Goal

Implement `jjf ready`: returns open issues with no open
`blocked-by` dependencies — the issues an agent (or human)
can pick up right now without waiting on anything else.
End state: `jjf ready --json --limit 1` returns one
unblocked issue; an agent runs that and immediately knows
what to work on next.

# Why now

This is the headline agent primitive `epic:agent-ergonomics`
exists to deliver. With it, the orchestration loop simplifies:
"read the roadmap" is replaced by "`jjf ready` runs the
unblocked query and the next thing happens." The MVP storage
+ CLI + sync are all in place; the dependency field already
exists on every issue; the enumeration primitive
(`Storage::list_ids`) already exists. `ready` is composing
what's there.

After this, `--claim` (atomic) is a follow-up ticket,
`remember` is a follow-up, the MCP server is a follow-up.
`ready` is the load-bearing verb that unblocks the rest.

# Scope

## Storage layer

- New `Storage::list_ready(filter: ReadyFilter) ->
  Result<Vec<Bug>>` (using current "Bug" types until
  the rename in `199ed91` lands).
- Implementation: walk `list_ids`, `read` each, filter out:
  - status != open
  - any `dependencies[]` id that is itself open in the
    set (it's blocking us)
  - any other filters: `--label X --label Y` (AND), per
    `ls`'s existing pattern.
- Return order: oldest first by `created_at` (FIFO —
  agents grind down the oldest unblocked work first).
- The full read-and-filter loop is fine for v1 (we
  already do read-all in `ls`). Index optimization is a
  follow-up.

## CLI

- New `Commands::Ready`.
- Args (clap derive):
  - `--label <NAME>` — repeatable, AND semantics
    (mirrors `ls`).
  - `--limit <N>` — optional, default unlimited.
    `--limit 1` is the agent's normal use.
  - Inherits global `--json`.
- Implementation:
  - Preflight: `bugs_bookmark` (needs the bookmark to
    exist).
  - Call `Storage::list_ready` with filters + limit.
- Plain-text output:
  - Same tab-separated rows as `ls`:
    `<id>\t<status>\t<label-count>L\t<title>` per line.
    No header. Empty result → exit 0, no output.
- `--json` output: array of `Bug` records (same shape
  as `ls --json`).
- Errors:
  - cwd not a jj repo → exit 2.
  - `bugs` bookmark missing → exit 2.
  - `Storage::list_ready` failure → exit 1.

## Docs

- `docs/cli-json.md`: new `ready` verb envelope (same
  array shape as `ls`).
- `CLAUDE.md` "Queries" section: add `jjf ready` /
  `jjf ready --limit 1` examples.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `Storage::list_ready(filter) -> Result<Vec<Bug>>`
  lands with storage tests covering:
  - 3 issues, none have deps → all returned (sorted
    oldest first).
  - 3 issues, issue B depends on A (open) → A and C
    returned, B excluded.
  - 3 issues, issue B depends on A which is CLOSED → all
    three returned (closed deps don't block).
  - Label filter intersection works as it does in `ls`.
  - Limit clamps the returned vec.
- [ ] `cargo build -p jjf` succeeds.
- [ ] CLI integration test: 3 issues with a dep chain,
  `jjf ready` returns only the unblocked ones.
- [ ] CLI integration test: `jjf ready --json --limit 1`
  returns a single-element JSON array.
- [ ] CLI integration test: `jjf ready --label backend`
  returns only matching unblocked issues.
- [ ] CLI integration test: nothing unblocked → exit 0,
  empty output / empty array.
- [ ] CLI integration test: preflight failures → exit 2
  with hints (mirroring `ls`).
- [ ] `jjf ready --help` documents `--label`, `--limit`,
  `--json`.
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` + `CLAUDE.md` updated.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green
  (currently 178).

# Out of scope

- **`--claim`**: atomic "give me the next one and set
  assignee=me" is its own follow-up ticket.
- A persistent index for fast `ready` over 10k+ issues.
- `--unblocked-by <id>`: "tell me what would become
  ready if X closes." Useful but follow-up.
- Hierarchical dependency walking. v1 is one hop only.
- MCP exposure of `ready` as a tool.

# Pointers

- Storage enumeration precedent: `Storage::list_ids` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`.
- Read API: `Storage::read(&BugId) -> Result<Bug>`.
- CLI `ls` impl: `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` `run_ls` —
  copy its structure, drop `--status` (always open
  here), apply the dep-filter inside the storage call.
- Dependencies field: `BugRecord.dependencies:
  Vec<BugId>` — semantic is "blocked-by."
- Beads' equivalent: `bd ready --limit 1`. Beads uses a
  SQLite index; we stay read-all until proven slow.
- Epic body: `5a755ec` (`epic:agent-ergonomics`).
